mission.title: k3s: Support cgroups v2
mission.description: Support cgroups v2. Requested by 10+ users.

steps.title: Check current k3s deployment
steps.description:
Verify your k3s version and configuration:
```bash
k3s --version
kubectl get nodes
```
This feature requires a working k3s installation.

steps.title: Review k3s configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant k3s configuration:
```bash
k3s --version
kubectl get nodes -o wide
kubectl get pods -A
```
`k3s-agent` is failing to run on Fedora 31. I suspect this is due to cgroups v2 being the default on Fedora 31, but can't quite figure out how to see which version of runc is shipping with k3s (runc recently landed support for cgroups

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support cgroups v2
steps.description:
This does not appear to be fully functional yet. On a Fedora 31 system, which uses Unified cgroups (full fledged cgroups v2), there is still a failure, though it does appear to get farther along in the process. Attached full
```yaml
Oct 15 07:41:51 mercury systemd[1]: k3s-agent.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Oct 15 07:41:51 mercury systemd[1]: k3s-agent.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=1/FAILURE
Oct 15 07:41:51 mercury k3s[23212]: time="2019-10-15T07:41:51.403568863-07:00" level=fatal msg="failed to find memory cgroup, you may need to add \"cgroup_memory=1 cgroup_enable=memory\" to your linux cmdline (/boot/cmdline.txt on a Rasp>
Oct 15 07:41:51 mercury k3s[23212]: time="2019-10-15T07:41:51.403559607-07:00" level=error msg="Failed to find memory cgroup, you may need to add \"cgroup_memory=1 cgro
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get nodes -o wide
kubectl get pods -A
kubectl get events -A --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support cgroups v2" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This does not appear to be fully functional yet. On a Fedora 31 system, which uses Unified cgroups (full fledged cgroups v2), there is still a failure, though it does appear to get farther along in the process. Attached full log:
[fedora31.log](https://github.com/k3s-io/k3s/files/5675856/fedora31.log).
It's a large file, so snippet included:
```
...

prerequisites.description: A running k3s cluster or a system where k3s can be installed.
